title: Wallet name uniqueness is global instead of per user
reasoning: Wallets are user-owned, and all wallet queries are scoped to request.user, but the model uniqueness constraint only includes name. This prevents different users from creating wallets with the same common name, such as 'Cash' or 'Bank', even though their wallets are otherwise isolated by user.
reproduction: User A creates a wallet named 'Cash'. User B then submits the wallet add form with name 'Cash'; the model uniqueness constraint rejects it.
recommendation: Change the wallet uniqueness constraint to include both user and name, and add a migration. Prefer a modern UniqueConstraint if supported by the Django version in use.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: Finances/tests.py has no wallet creation or multi-user uniqueness tests.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a test showing two different users can create wallets with the same name, while one user cannot create duplicate wallet names.
minimumFixScope: Update Wallet.Meta uniqueness and create the corresponding migration.
